content: The intrinsic reward signal generated by ICM for autonomous agents can be repurposed to actively prune low-value information from an LLM agent's working memory, proactively avoiding reasoning degradation caused by working memory capacity limits. This creates a self-regulating loop where the agent's own curiosity drives it to keep only novel, high-reward information in working memory, rather than being overwhelmed by redundant or irrelevant long-range context.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
